payload.description: MTA Corporate Governance Committee Meeting at 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room, 12:00 - 12:45 PM ET
MTA Corporate Governance Committee Meeting at 2 Broadway - 20th Floor, New York, NY. Called to order at 1:18 P.M., adjourned at 1:28pm.

payload.claim_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) preserves and enhances the quality of life and economic health of the region it serves through the cost-efficient provision of safe, on-time, reliable, and clean transportation services.

tool_args.label: MTA 2023 Strategic Priorities and Performance Measures

payload.claim_text: The MTA's strategic priorities are: Deliver better service, Promote safety & respect, Increase appeal for customers, Provide 21st century bus service, Achieve financial stability & viability, Strengthen & expand the network, Revive talent & culture. Each priority is monitored by key performance measures as listed in the 2023 Mission Statement, Measurements, and Performance Indicators Report.
payload.subject: MTA

tool_args.label: No proposed revisions to Procurement Guidelines

payload.claim_text: There are no proposed revisions to either guidelines.
payload.subject: All Agency Procurement Guidelines and All Agency Guidelines for Procurement of Services

payload.claim_text: Committee approved revisions to the Governance Guidelines, including (1) assuring the guidelines are gender neutral and (2) changing JCOPE to COELIG to comply with the Ethics Commission Reform Act of 2022.
payload.subject: MTA Governance Guidelines

tool_args.label: Code of Ethics Zero-Tolerance Gift Policy

payload.claim_text: Employees are prohibited from soliciting or receiving Gifts, directly or indirectly, from any Prohibited Source. The defined term 'Gift' does not include items of truly nominal value.
payload.subject: MTA Employees

payload.claim_text: Policy 11-041 encourages reporting of wrongful acts by MTA employees, protects whistleblowers from retaliation/adverse action, and prohibits knowingly false reports. Effective October 20, 2021.
payload.subject: MTA Employees

tool_args.label: MTA Code of Ethics Originally Adopted 2005

payload.claim_text: The MTA Code of Ethics was originally adopted May 25, 2005, and revised on: March 29, 2006; March 28, 2007; December 16, 2009; November 16, 2011; February 26, 2014; November 19, 2014; December 16, 2015; and March 29, 2023.
payload.subject: MTA Code of Ethics

payload.claim_text: The Board Members Code of Ethics was originally issued March 2006 and revised March 2007, December 2009, November 2011, November 2014, and March 2023.
payload.subject: MTA Board Members Code of Ethics

Lamond Kearse is Chief Compliance Officer

payload.claim_text: The All Agency General Contract Procurement Guidelines and Service Contract Procurement Guidelines apply to: MTA, NYCT, LIRR, MNR, SIRTOA, MaBSTOA, MTAC&D, MTA Bus Company, FMTAC, GCMC, and Bridges and Tunnels.
payload.subject: MTA Procurement Guidelines

payload.claim_text: The All Agency General Contract Procurement Guidelines and All Agency Service Contract Procurement Guidelines were adopted by the Board on March 29, 2023.
payload.subject: MTA Procurement Guidelines

tool_args.label: Mission Statement Report Submitted as Part of MTA 2023 Annual Report

payload.claim_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority Mission Statement, Measurements, and Performance Indicators Report covering Fiscal Year 2023 was submitted in compliance with New York State Public Authorities Law §2800 and §2824-a as part of the MTA 2023 Annual Report to the Governor.
payload.subject: MTA 2023 Annual Report
